title_en: The Sharh's Legacy: From Madrasa to Modern Study
content_en:
The Sharh of an-Nawawi on Sahih Muslim entered the Islamic educational curriculum quickly and never left. By the eighth Islamic century — barely a generation after an-Nawawi's death — copies of the commentary were circulating widely across the Muslim world, and it had acquired the status of an authoritative reference that later works on Sahih Muslim would need to engage. This rapid adoption reflects both the quality of the work and the absence of serious competition: no other commentary on Muslim's Sahih matched the Sharh's combination of depth, clarity, and coverage.

In the madrasa tradition, the Sharh served multiple functions. As a commentary on a canonical hadith collection, it was used to teach the content of Sahih Muslim to advanced students. As a work of Shafi'i fiqh rooted in hadith evidence, it supplemented the legal curriculum. As an example of scholarly method — balancing transmission, legal reasoning, linguistic analysis, and theological awareness — it provided a model for students learning how to approach classical texts.

The Ottoman scholarly tradition gave the Sharh particular prominence. Turkish scholars produced glosses and marginal commentaries on sections of the work, and it featured prominently in the curricula of major Ottoman religious institutions. The Ottoman period also saw the production of many of the manuscript copies that are now held in libraries across Turkey, Egypt, and the Levant, testament to the work's continuous use through five centuries of Islamic institutional education.

In South Asia, where hadith scholarship developed with exceptional intensity from the twelfth Islamic century onward, the Sharh occupied a central place alongside the Fath al-Bari. Scholars of both the Deobandi and Barelwi traditions studied it, and it generated its own tradition of commentary and annotation in Urdu and Persian. The great hadith scholars of the subcontinent — figures like Shah Waliullah Dehlawi and later scholars of both traditions — engaged an-Nawawi's positions carefully, sometimes agreeing, sometimes offering corrections rooted in their own scholarly priorities.

Modern printed editions of the Sharh are plentiful. The edition published by Dar Ihya' at-Turath al-Arabi in Beirut, with its clear typesetting and useful marginalia, became a standard text in the twentieth century. More recent editions have added indices, cross-references to other hadith collections, and notes identifying the works an-Nawawi cited, making the commentary more accessible to modern researchers who lack the full range of classical library resources at hand.

For English-speaking students, portions of the Sharh have been translated, though no complete English translation exists. The Nawawi Foundation and various academic projects have produced translations of specific books within the Sharh, particularly the Book of Faith, and these give non-Arabic readers a genuine taste of the commentary's method and scope.

The Sharh of an-Nawawi remains what it was when it first appeared: the indispensable companion to Sahih Muslim for any serious student of hadith. Its longevity in the curriculum of traditional Islamic learning is a measure of how thoroughly an-Nawawi understood what students of his collection needed and how well he met that need.
title_id: Warisan Syarh: Dari Madrasah ke Studi Modern
content_id:
Syarh al-Nawawi atas Sahih Muslim masuk ke dalam kurikulum pendidikan Islam dengan cepat dan tidak pernah pergi. Pada abad kedelapan Hijriah — hanya satu generasi setelah wafatnya al-Nawawi — salinan-salinan komentar itu beredar luas di seluruh dunia Muslim, dan ia telah memperoleh status sebagai referensi otoritatif yang karya-karya kemudian tentang Sahih Muslim perlu terlibat dengannya. Adopsi cepat ini mencerminkan baik kualitas karya itu maupun tidak adanya persaingan yang serius: tidak ada komentar lain tentang Sahih Muslim yang menandingi kombinasi kedalaman, kejelasan, dan cakupan dari Syarh.

Dalam madrasah-madrasah tradisional, Syarh al-Nawawi digunakan dalam beberapa cara berbeda tergantung pada tingkat pelajar. Untuk pemula, pengantar al-Nawawi untuk setiap buku dan pernyataan tematisnya yang ringkas adalah sumber yang sangat berharga untuk memahami topik-topik apa yang ditangani Sahih Muslim. Untuk pelajar menengah, analisis linguistik dan yurisprudensinya dari hadis-hadis individual memberikan model tentang bagaimana melakukan analisis teks yang cermat. Untuk para ulama tingkat lanjut, terlibat dengan penilaian-penilaiannya yang lebih kontroversial dan di mana mereka berbeda pendapat dengannya adalah bagian dari pengembangan kemerdekaan keilmuan.

Syarh juga dipengaruhi dan dikomentari oleh generasi-generasi ulama setelahnya. Beberapa karya yang paling signifikan dalam studi hadis — termasuk Al-Mu'lim karya al-Maziri, Ikmal al-Mu'lim karya Qadhi 'Iyadh, dan Ikmal al-Ikmal karya Ibnu Khalifah al-Ubbi — terlibat langsung dengan Syarh, baik membangunnya maupun kadang-kadang menantangnya. Percakapan keilmuan yang dihasilkan oleh warisan al-Nawawi tentang Muslim mencerminkan cara tradisi hadis Sunni berfungsi: sebagai akumulasi yang berkelanjutan dan penghalusan pemahaman melalui keterlibatan beralasan dengan teks-teks otoritatif.

Di era modern, Syarh al-Nawawi telah dialihkan ke format digital dan tersedia secara luas melalui berbagai platform. Ini telah membuatnya lebih mudah diakses oleh pelajar yang tidak memiliki akses ke madrasah tradisional, tetapi juga telah menghadirkan risiko baru: pembacaan yang tidak terbimbing dari karya yang sangat teknis dapat menyebabkan salah paham. Teks ini ditulis untuk pelajar yang sudah memiliki latar belakang substansial dalam bahasa Arab, tata bahasa, fiqh, dan ilmu hadis — tanpa latar belakang itu, detail-detail teknisnya tidak hanya sulit tetapi juga berpotensi menyesatkan.

Bagi pelajar Muslim kontemporer, nilai Syarh al-Nawawi terletak tidak hanya pada konten spesifiknya tetapi pada model keilmuan yang ia contohkan. Dalam dunia di mana teks-teks keagamaan sering didekati baik dengan penerimaan tidak kritis atau penolakan gegabah, karya al-Nawawi menunjukkan jalan yang ketiga: keterlibatan yang penuh hormat, hati-hati, dan substantif dengan teks-teks otoritatif yang siap untuk menyimpulkan dengan mengikuti bukti ke mana pun ia mengarah. Ini adalah tipe keilmuan yang dapat dicontoh oleh setiap Muslim terdidik, terlepas dari apakah mereka pernah membuka teks al-Nawawi sendiri.
content_ar: The Sharh of النووي on صحيح مسلم entered the Islamic educational curriculum quickly and never left. By the ثامناً Islamic century — barely a generation بعد النووي's الموت — copies of the commentary were circulating widely across the مسلم الدنيا, and it had acquired the status of an المعتمد reference that later works on صحيح مسلم would need to engage. This rapid adoption reflects both the quality of the work and the absence of serious competition: no other commentary on مسلم's صحيح matched the Sharh's combination of depth, clarity, and coverage. In the madrasa التراث, the Sharh served multiple functions. As a commentary on a canonical الحديث collection, it was used to teach the content of صحيح مسلم to advanced students. As a work of الشافعي الفقه rooted in الحديث evidence, it supplemented the legal curriculum. As an example of العلمي method — balancing transmission, legal reasoning, linguistic analysis, and theological awareness — it provided a model for students learning how to approach الكلاسيكي texts. The Ottoman العلمي التراث gave the Sharh particular prominence. Turkish العلماء produced glosses and marginal commentaries on sections of the work, and it featured prominently in the curricula of major Ottoman religious institutions. The Ottoman period أيضاً saw the production of many of the manuscript copies that are now held in libraries across Turkey, Egypt, and the Levant, testament to the work's continuous use through five centuries of Islamic institutional education. In South Asia, where الحديث scholarship developed with exceptional intensity from the twelfth Islamic century onward, the Sharh occupied a المحوري place alongside the Fath al-Bari. العلماء of both the Deobandi and Barelwi traditions studied it, and it generated its own التراث of commentary and annotation in Urdu and Persian. The great الحديث العلماء of the subcontinent — figures like Shah Waliullah Dehlawi and later العلماء of both traditions — engaged النووي's positions carefully, sometimes agreeing, sometimes offering corrections rooted in their own العلمي priorities. Modern printed editions of the Sharh are plentiful. The edition published by Dar Ihya' at-Turath al-Arabi in Beirut, with its clear typesetting and useful marginalia, became a standard text in the twentieth century. More recent editions have added indices, cross-references to other الحديث collections, and notes identifying the works النووي cited, making the commentary more accessible to modern researchers who lack the full range of الكلاسيكي library resources at hand. For English-speaking students, portions of the Sharh have been translated, though no complete English translation exists. The Nawawi Foundation and various الأكاديمي projects have produced translations of specific الكتب within the Sharh, particularly the الكتاب of الإيمان, and these give non-Arabic readers a genuine taste of the commentary's method and scope. <
